title: 6 AgriInvest Account Activity
body_text:
6	AgriInvest Account Activity
6.1	Overview of AgriInvest Account Activity
All account activity must be recorded on the AgriInvest Activity File and submitted to Central 1 semi-monthly.
Note:	All AgriInvest Accounts must be reported on the Activity File, even if there is no activity on the account.
Deposits to AgriInvest Accounts must be made by the deposit deadline shown on the AgriInvest Deposit Notice. Only one deposit can be made per AgriInvest Deposit Notice. Interest can be deposited at the Financial Institutionâs discretion, but the minimum requirement is once a year, unless the Participant declines interest payments. Withdrawals can be made by the Participant at any time.
6.2	Processing Deposits
Participants make deposits based on a percentage of their Allowable Net Sales. Financial Institutions arenât responsible for determining the eligibility of deposits to AgriInvest Accounts. However, Financial Institutions are responsible for the timely and accurate processing of deposits to the AgriInvest Account and are responsible for any lost benefits resulting from their errors.
Note:	An account transfer doesnât extend the deposit deadline. An account transfer should be requested well in advance of the deposit deadline to ensure that the new AgriInvest Account can be opened and the deposit posted on time.
When there is an excess amount in an AgriInvest Account, the AgriInvest Administration contacts the Participant with instructions to withdraw the excess amount. If the Participant doesnât comply within the specified time period, AgriInvest Administration will issue a Letter of Direction to the Financial Institution with instructions to pay out the excess amount.
To process a deposit to an AgriInvest Account:
Deposit the Participantâs contribution to the AgriInvest Account as per the Financial Institutionâs regular procedures.
Record the deposit on the AgriInvest Activity File.
For more information on reporting account details, see Chapter 7, Reporting to Central 1.
6.3	Processing Withdrawals
Participants can make withdrawals from their AgriInvest Accounts at any time. Withdrawals are first paid from Fund 2 (government contributions and interest). Once Fund 2 is depleted, withdrawals are issued from Fund 1 (Participantâs contributions).
To process a withdrawal from an AgriInvest Account:
Withdraw the requested amount (up to the amount of funds available).
Record the withdrawal on the AgriInvest Activity File.
For more information on reporting account details, see Chapter 7, Reporting to Central 1.
Note:	A Participant can withdraw all available funds from the AgriInvest Account without having to close the account. AgriInvest Accounts are only closed upon specific request. For more information, see Section 5.4, Closing an AgriInvest Account.
6.4	Processing Transfers
An account transfer doesnât extend the deposit deadline. An account transfer should be requested well in advance of the deposit deadline to ensure that the new AgriInvest Account can be opened and the deposit posted on time.
Transfers to Another Financial Institution
AgriInvest funds must be transferred within seven days of the new Financial Institution receiving the transfer request. When your Financial Institution receives a request for transfer of AgriInvest funds from another Financial Institution:
Close the Participantâs AgriInvest Account.
Forward the payment to the new Financial Institution.
Record the final withdrawal amount and the closing date of the account with a zero balance on the AgriInvest Activity File.
For more information on reporting account details, see Chapter 7, Reporting to Central 1.
For procedures on handling transfers
from
another Financial Institution, see Section 5.3, Opening an AgriInvest Account to Receive a Transfer.
Branch Closures/Mergers/Transfers Initiated by the Financial Institution
The Financial Institution must provide the AgriInvest Administration with at least one-month notification prior to implementation if the AgriInvest Account numbers will be changed as a result of a branch closure, merger, or transfer.
AAFC will suspend the accounts to ensure that the fund balances are properly transferred over to the new account number reported on the Activity File and block new matching contributions from being issued until the change in account number is complete. The previous account number must show a final withdrawal and closure date before AAFC allows the new account number to open and accept the deposit of the funds transferred from the old account. AAFC Administration will assess if AgriInvest Account Transfer Forms have to be sent to Participants to reflect the changes to ensure proper consent is obtained for ongoing transactions with the new AgriInvest Accounts.
Central 1 manages transit number changes related to branch closures, mergers, or transfers initiated by the Financial Institution. To request a transit number change, notify the AgriInvest Officer at Central 1, prior to implementation. See Section 1.4, Contacts and Websites. Provide the old and the new transit numbers, and, for Central 1âs recordkeeping purposes, include notice of account number changes. Report the new transit number on the AgriInvest Activity File.
Transferring an Individual Account to a Corporation
Members can transfer an individual account to a corporation if the AgriInvest Account is stipulated in the assets transferred by incorporation. The process to transfer an individual account to a corporate account is as follows:
An authorized officer from the corporation brings a completed and signed AgriInvest Account Initiation Form to the Financial Institution.
The Administration sends the Financial Institution a Letter of Direction to close the individual account and transfer the funds to the corporate AgriInvest Account.
The Financial Institution reports the account details for the corporation on the Activity File.
Note:	The deposit deadline on an AgriInvest Deposit Notice must continue to be met for the corporate account to receive matching government contributions. If a deposit is made to the individual account before the account transfer request, the matching government contributions wonât be credited to the corporate account until the transfer process is complete. However, deposits can be made to the corporate account before the individual account is transferred to ensure the deposit is posted on time.
For more information on reporting account details, see Chapter 7, Reporting to Central 1.
6.5	Interest Payments and Service Charges on AgriInvest Accounts
Interest rates on AgriInvest Accounts are set by negotiation between the member and the Financial Institution. Interest is earned on deposits from the date the funds are deposited into the AgriInvest Account. Interest earned on the AgriInvest Account must be paid out no less frequently than once per year. Interest earned on the AgriInvest Account is credited to Fund 2 of the account.
Record interest deposits on the AgriInvest Activity File. For more information on reporting account details, see Chapter 7, Reporting to Central 1.
Service Charges
Donât debit the AgriInvest Account for service charges, since withdrawals from the AgriInvest Account come out of Fund 2 first and are treated as a taxable transaction. Either debit another account held by the Participant or invoice the Participant for the charges.
END
